subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference
- **Standard game** — 4 to 10 players (must have at least 2 per team).
- **Recommended** — 8 players (two teams of 4).

#### Core Components
- **Taboo cards** — Over 1,000 cards, each with a guess word and 5 taboo (forbidden) words.
- **Card holder** — 1 card holder/tray for the active deck.
- **Buzzer** — 1 squeaker buzzer for the opposing team's monitor.
- **Sand timer** — 1 sixty-second timer.
- **Score pad and pencil** — For tracking team scores.
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.

#### Card Layout
- **Top of card** — The guess word in large text.
- **Below guess word** — 5 taboo words listed that the clue-giver cannot say.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Form teams** — Divide players into two teams as evenly as possible.
2. **Load card holder** — Place the deck of Taboo cards in the card holder.
3. **Position buzzer** — Give the buzzer to the opposing team's designated monitor.
4. **Prepare timer** — Place the sand timer within easy reach.
5. **Prepare scorepad** — Set up two columns on the score pad, one per team.

#### Seating
- **Clue-giver position** — The active clue-giver sits or stands where their team can hear them.
- **Monitor position** — One member of the opposing team sits next to the clue-giver to read the taboo words and watch for violations.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Team Roles
- **Clue-giver** — Rotates within each team each turn.
- **Guessers** — All other members of the clue-giver's team.
- **Monitor** — One member of the opposing team watches the clue-giver for taboo word violations and operates the buzzer.

#### Role Rotation
- **Clue-giver rotation** — After each turn, the clue-giver role passes to the next teammate.
- **Monitor rotation** — The monitor role also rotates within the opposing team.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Pre-First-Turn Checks
- **Teams formed** — Two teams with first clue-givers and monitors identified.
- **Cards loaded** — Deck is in the card holder.
- **Timer and buzzer ready** — Both within reach.

#### First Action
1. **Determine starting team** — Any method (coin flip, etc.).
2. **Start timer** — Flip the 60-second timer.
3. **Begin cluing** — Clue-giver draws the first card and begins describing the guess word.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Flow
1. **Start timer** — Flip the 60-second sand timer.
2. **Draw card** — Clue-giver takes the top card from the holder.
3. **Give clues** — Describe the guess word without saying any of the 5 taboo words, the guess word itself, or any part/form of those words.
4. **Team guesses** — Teammates call out guesses freely. No penalty for wrong guesses.
5. **Correct guess** — Set the card aside in the team's score pile. Immediately draw the next card.
6. **Continue** — Keep drawing and cluing until the timer runs out.
7. **Timer expires** — Turn ends immediately. Discard any card in progress.
8. **Score the turn** — Count correct guesses, subtract penalties.
9. **Switch teams** — Opposing team takes their turn.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Clue-Giver Rules
- **Allowed** — Use any words, phrases, or sentences that are not taboo.
- **Allowed** — Use gestures and sounds alongside verbal clues.
- **Forbidden** — Say the guess word or any part of it.
- **Forbidden** — Say any of the 5 listed taboo words or any part of them.
- **Forbidden** — Use "sounds like" or rhyming clues referencing the word.
- **Forbidden** — Use abbreviations or acronyms of taboo words.
- **Forbidden** — Use "blank" as a placeholder for the word.

#### Monitor Actions
- **Watch and listen** — Monitor reads the card over the clue-giver's shoulder.
- **Buzz** — Press the buzzer immediately if the clue-giver says a taboo word, the guess word, or breaks any clue-giving rule.
- **Buzz effect** — The current card is discarded (counts as a penalty). Clue-giver draws the next card.

#### Pass Option
- **Pass allowed** — Clue-giver may choose to pass on a difficult card.
- **Pass penalty** — The passed card counts as a penalty (minus 1 point).

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Buzzer Mechanic
- **Immediate effect** — When buzzed, the clue-giver must stop cluing that card instantly.
- **Penalty** — The buzzed card goes to the penalty pile (minus 1 point from the team's score for the turn).
- **Disputes** — If the clue-giver disputes a buzz, the group decides. If overruled, the card returns to play.

#### Scoring per Turn
- **Correct guesses** — +1 point per correctly guessed card.
- **Penalties** — -1 point per buzzed card or passed card.
- **Net score** — Correct guesses minus penalties = turn score. Minimum turn score is 0 (cannot go negative in most editions).

#### Game Variants
- **Speed Taboo** — Reduce timer to 30 seconds for a faster-paced game.
- **All Play** — Both teams can guess simultaneously on certain cards.
- **House rules** — Many groups skip the pass penalty or allow it without cost.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- **Predetermined rounds** — Play a set number of rounds (typically each player is clue-giver once).
- **Alternatively** — Play until the card deck runs out or to a target score.

#### Final Scoring
- **Tally** — Sum each team's net points across all turns.
- **Winner** — Team with the most total points wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **No official tiebreaker** — Play a sudden-death round where each team gets one 60-second turn; higher net score wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Clue-Giver Priorities
- **Read taboo words first** — Quickly scan all 5 taboo words before speaking to avoid accidental violations.
- **Find the angle** — Identify a description path that avoids all taboo words.
- **Start broad, narrow fast** — Give a category or context clue first, then zero in with specifics.
- **Keep moving** — If your team is stuck after 5-10 seconds, consider passing to preserve time for easier cards.

#### Team Priorities
- **Shout freely** — Wrong guesses cost nothing; volume of guesses increases hit rate.
- **Listen for context** — Pay attention to the clue-giver's sentence structure for embedded hints.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Not reading taboo words** — Speaking before scanning the forbidden list leads to instant buzzes.
- **Spending too long on one card** — Burning 20+ seconds on a hard card wastes time that could yield 2-3 easier cards.
- **Word-form slips** — Saying a derivative of a taboo word (e.g., saying "swimming" when "swim" is taboo).
- **Passive guessing** — Teammates who wait for perfect clues instead of rapid-firing guesses slow the team down.
- **Monitor leniency** — Monitors who are too slow to buzz let violations slide and disadvantage their own team.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Pass or Persist
- **Pass** — Accept the -1 penalty to move to a potentially easier card. Best when more than 3 taboo words block your usual description.
- **Persist** — Invest time in a difficult card if you have a creative angle. Worth it for obscure words your team might know.

#### Decision 2: Clue Specificity
- **Specific clue** — Gets the answer faster but risks accidentally using a taboo word.
- **Roundabout clue** — Safer but slower; use when the direct path is blocked by taboo words.

#### Decision 3: Monitoring Strictness
- **Strict monitoring** — Buzz immediately on any borderline violation. Maximizes your team's advantage.
- **Lenient monitoring** — Letting minor slips go keeps the game fun but may cost your team points.
